content.problemStatement:
**Please note that this problem has subtasks**.

You are given a string SSS of length NNN.

In one operation, you can select a **non-empty** prefix and a **non-empty** suffix which do **not intersect**; and swap them.

For example let S=S =S= `avfdgs`. In one operation, you can choose prefix of length 222 and suffix of length 333, and swap them to make S=S =S= `dgsfav`.

Your task it to **sort** the string in **at most** KKK operations. Note that you don't need to **minimise** the number of operations.

Output your scheme. It can be proven the scheme always exists. If multiple schemes exist, you may print any.

A prefix is a substring formed by deleting some characters from the end of the string while a suffix is a substring formed by deleting some characters from the beginning of the string.


Input Format

The first line contains the number of test cases TTT. The description of the test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two positive integers NNN and KKK - the length of string and maximum number of allowed operations.
The second line of each test case contains a string SSS containing lowercase english alphabets.


Output Format
For each test case, print P+1P+1P+1 lines:



The first line contains an integer P (0≤P≤K)P \ (0 \leq P \leq K)P (0≤P≤K), the number of operations.
The next PPP lines each contain two space separated integers X,Y (1≤X,Y≤N−1,X+Y≤N)X,Y \ (1\leq X,Y \leq N-1,X+Y \leq N)X,Y (1≤X,Y≤N−1,X+Y≤N) denoting that you choose prefix of length XXX and suffix of length YYY.

It can be proven the scheme always exists. If multiple schemes exist, you may print any.


**Note that you don't need to minimise the number of operations.**
constraints: SSS consists of lowercase english alphabets.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500050005000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The string is already sorted. Thus, we need 000 operations.
Test case 222: We can sort the string in 111 operation:

Choose prefix and suffix of length 111 and swap it.

Test case 333: We can sort the string in 222 operations:

Choose prefix of length 111 and suffix of length 222 and swap it. Thus, SSS becomes cab.
Choose prefix of length 111 and suffix of length 222 and swap it. Thus, SSS becomes abc.
